verses.text: An angel cries with great power, 'Babylon the great is fallen,' become a hold of devils, for all nations drank her wine. Fallen. The proud city is utterly ruined.
An angel cries with great power, 'Babylon the great is fallen,' become a hold of devils, for all nations drank her wine. Fallen. The proud city is utterly ruined.
An angel cries with great power, 'Babylon the great is fallen,' become a hold of devils, for all nations drank her wine. Fallen. The proud city is utterly ruined.
'Come out of her, my people, that ye be not partakers of her sins,' for her sins 'have reached unto heaven.' Come out. God calls His own to separate from her.
'Come out of her, my people, that ye be not partakers of her sins,' for her sins 'have reached unto heaven.' Come out. God calls His own to separate from her.
Reward her double; she said, 'I sit a queen... and shall see no sorrow,' but her plagues come in one day. Sudden fall. Her boast meets swift judgment.
Reward her double; she said, 'I sit a queen... and shall see no sorrow,' but her plagues come in one day. Sudden fall. Her boast meets swift judgment.
Reward her double; she said, 'I sit a queen... and shall see no sorrow,' but her plagues come in one day. Sudden fall. Her boast meets swift judgment.
Kings who lived in her luxury bewail her, standing afar, crying, 'Alas... for in one hour is thy judgment come.' Kings mourn. Her partners weep at her ruin.
Kings who lived in her luxury bewail her, standing afar, crying, 'Alas... for in one hour is thy judgment come.' Kings mourn. Her partners weep at her ruin.
The merchants weep, for none buys their goods — gold, silks, spices, and 'slaves, and souls of men.' Trade ends. Her wealth and traffic are gone.
The merchants weep, for none buys their goods — gold, silks, spices, and 'slaves, and souls of men.' Trade ends. Her wealth and traffic are gone.
The merchants weep, for none buys their goods — gold, silks, spices, and 'slaves, and souls of men.' Trade ends. Her wealth and traffic are gone.
The fruits she desired are gone; merchants who grew rich stand afar, weeping, 'in one hour so great riches is come to nought.' Vanished wealth. Riches perish in a moment.
The fruits she desired are gone; merchants who grew rich stand afar, weeping, 'in one hour so great riches is come to nought.' Vanished wealth. Riches perish in a moment.
The fruits she desired are gone; merchants who grew rich stand afar, weeping, 'in one hour so great riches is come to nought.' Vanished wealth. Riches perish in a moment.
The fruits she desired are gone; merchants who grew rich stand afar, weeping, 'in one hour so great riches is come to nought.' Vanished wealth. Riches perish in a moment.
Shipmasters cry, casting dust on their heads, 'What city is like unto this great city!' for in one hour she is desolate. Sailors mourn. All who profited lament her end.
Shipmasters cry, casting dust on their heads, 'What city is like unto this great city!' for in one hour she is desolate. Sailors mourn. All who profited lament her end.
'Rejoice over her, thou heaven... for God hath avenged you on her.' Heaven rejoices. The saints' wrongs are righted at last.

A mighty angel casts a millstone into the sea: 'Thus... shall Babylon be thrown down,' found no more, for in her was 'the blood of... all that were slain.' Gone forever. The city of blood sinks without trace.
